order_comment: Comprehensive credit intelligence report for Infinite Mining and Energy FZE based on financial statements 2022-2024 and company questionnaire
trade_names: Previously known as Infinite Energy FZE
company_type: Free Zone Establishment with Limited Liability
company_address: Plot Nos. MRT-19, MRT-20, MRT-22, MRT-21A, MRT-21B and MRT-22, Phase 2, Hamriyah Free Zone, Sharjah, UAE
headquarters_address: Plot Nos. MRT-19, MRT-20, MRT-22, MRT-21A, MRT-21B and MRT-22, Phase 2, Hamriyah Free Zone, Sharjah, UAE
auditor_name: Baker Tilly MKM Chartered Accountants (2024-2025); Behl, Lad & Al Sayegh Chartered Accountants (2022-2023)
industry: Petroleum products, petrochemicals, and minerals trading
executive_summary_text: Infinite Mining and Energy FZE is a UAE-based trading company specializing in petroleum products, petrochemicals, and minerals. Established in 2018 in Hamriyah Free Zone, the company has demonstrated strong growth with revenue increasing from AED 262M (2022) to AED 497M (2024), representing 90% growth. Net profit grew from AED 6.3M to AED 11.9M over the same period. The company operates with minimal share capital (AED 11,000) but has built substantial operational capacity including a 1,000 MT per day refinery and 27-tank storage infrastructure with 50,000 MT capacity. Sole proprietor Mr. Bilal Iqbal Merchant (Indian national) maintains 100% ownership and serves as Managing Director.
company_history_text: Founded in July 2018 as a Limited Liability Free Zone Establishment in Hamriyah Free Zone, Sharjah. The company initially focused on petroleum product trading and has since expanded into refining operations. In 2023, the company obtained Industrial License No. 25842 and acquired multiple plots (MRT-19, MRT-20, MRT-21A, MRT-21B, MRT-22) for refinery and tankage facilities. The company transitioned from third-party auditors in 2023 to Baker Tilly MKM in 2024, reflecting institutional maturity.
affiliates: Wonder Star Trading LLC (80% ownership by related party)

regional_affiliates.affiliate_name: Wonder Star Trading LLC (80% related party ownership)

registration_activities_description: Import/export/trading in petroleum products, petrochemicals, and minerals
activities_full_description: The principal activity of the Establishment is import/export/trading in petroleum products, petrochemicals, and minerals. The Establishment derives its revenue mainly from trading and processing of petrochemicals and petroleum products. The company operates a refinery with 1,000 MT per day capacity and storage infrastructure comprising 27 tanks with total capacity of 50,000 MT. Products include minerals (coal and clinker), refined petroleum products, and petrochemical products.
facilities_count: Multiple plots comprising office, factory, and warehouse facilities
markets_regions: Middle East, Asia, Europe, North America, UK
main_suppliers: Gunvor Middle East DMCC/Gunvor SA (UAE/Switzerland), Chemlube SA (Switzerland), Trafigura Pte Ltd (Singapore)
import_countries: Malaysia, Singapore, USA, Taiwan, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, China
import_items: Petroleum products, petrochemicals, minerals, base oils, additives
key_customers: Nirva Energy Ltd (UK), Texol Lubritech FZC (UAE), Green Petrochem Industry FZC (UAE), Sharjah National Lube Oil Company LLC (UAE), Aachim Energy FZE (UAE), Thar Oil Refinery FZC (UAE), Oceanic Oil Power FZC (UAE), Terramont FZE (UAE)

banking_relationships.facility_type: Multiple facilities including term loan, trust receipt, local bill discounting

banking_notes: Company has established relationships with 5 major UAE banks. Total interest-bearing loans and borrowings as of Dec 2024: AED 63.0M comprising term loan (AED 16.7M), trust receipt (AED 35.9M), and local bill discounting (AED 10.3M).
current_ratio_interpretation: Current ratio declined from 1.56 to 1.18 year-over-year due to increased short-term borrowings supporting business expansion. Still above 1.0 but below industry benchmark, requiring monitoring.
quick_ratio_interpretation: Quick ratio deteriorated significantly from 1.44 to 0.87 due to large inventory build-up (AED 31.4M vs AED 5.2M). This reflects the refinery operations but indicates potential liquidity pressure.
cash_ratio_interpretation: Cash ratio declined from 0.36 to 0.17, below industry average. Cash increased but current liabilities grew faster, suggesting reliance on operating cash conversion and credit facilities.
gross_margin_interpretation: Gross margin improved from 3.79% to 4.47%, indicating better pricing or cost management. However, remains significantly below industry average of 8%, reflecting competitive commodity trading environment.
ebitda_margin_interpretation: EBITDA margin improved to 4.23% from 3.72%, demonstrating operational leverage and cost control despite rapid growth. Below industry average but trending positively.
net_margin_interpretation: Net margin of 2.39% reflects thin margins typical of commodity trading. Slight improvement from 2.28% indicates stable profitability despite revenue growth and increased financing costs.
roa_interpretation: ROA declined from 7.50% to 6.40% as asset base expanded faster than profits. This reflects capital-intensive expansion (refinery and tanks) with returns expected to improve as utilization increases.
roe_interpretation: ROE improved from 15.06% to 18.84%, exceeding industry benchmark. This demonstrates effective use of shareholder equity and leverage to generate returns. Strong performance for a trading company.
debt_equity_interpretation: Debt-to-equity ratio surged from 0.07 to 0.99 as company took on AED 63M in interest-bearing debt to fund expansion. Now slightly above industry average, indicating increased financial risk but still manageable.
debt_assets_interpretation: Debt-to-assets ratio increased from 4% to 34% but remains below industry average of 40%. Company maintains 66% equity financing, providing reasonable financial cushion.
equity_ratio_interpretation: Equity ratio declined from 49.82% to 34.00% as company increased leverage for growth. Still above minimum prudent levels but below industry average, reflecting more aggressive capital structure.
interest_coverage_interpretation: Interest coverage dropped significantly from 50.5x to 6.6x due to AED 3.2M in new interest expenses. Still adequate to cover obligations 6.6 times but requires monitoring as debt service has increased materially.
asset_turnover_interpretation: Asset turnover declined from 3.28x to 2.68x as major capital investments (refinery, tanks) were made. Turnover should improve as new assets reach full utilization. Currently below industry benchmark.
dio_interpretation: Days inventory outstanding increased from 7 to 24 days, still well below industry average of 30 days. Reflects transition to refining (requiring working inventory) while maintaining efficient inventory management.
dso_interpretation: Days sales outstanding improved from 48 to 37 days, better than industry average of 45 days. Demonstrates strong collection performance despite offering 30-90 day credit terms to customers.
dpo_interpretation: Days payable outstanding extended from 30 to 36 days, approaching industry average of 40 days. Company is managing payment terms effectively while maintaining supplier relationships. Most suppliers require advance payment.
ccc_interpretation: Cash conversion cycle remained stable at 25 days, significantly better than industry average of 35 days. This demonstrates efficient working capital management despite business expansion and reflects strong operational execution.
viability_meaning: Company demonstrates strong revenue growth and profitability with adequate capitalization. Increased leverage and working capital intensity present moderate risks.
delinquency_meaning: Strong payment history, improving collections (DSO 37 days), and adequate liquidity suggest low probability of payment delays. Banking relationships are well-established.
failure_meaning: Strong equity base and cash generation indicate low probability of business failure
payment_meaning: Strong payment performance with 85% on-time payments and improving DSO

alerts.alert_message: Debt levels increased significantly in 2024 (AED 63M vs near-zero in 2023). Monitor debt servicing capacity.
Major capital expansion underway: 1,000 MT/day refinery and 50,000 MT storage capacity. Capital work-in-progress AED 30.6M.
Revenue growth of 65% year-over-year with improving profitability margins demonstrates strong market position.
Current ratio declined from 1.56 to 1.18. Inventory increased 6x to AED 31.4M, indicating operational shift to refining.
Company operates with minimal share capital (AED 11K) and single shareholder concentration. Strong retained earnings of AED 37.2M.

news_events.event_title: Major Capital Expansion - Refinery and Storage Infrastructure
news_events.event_summary: Company invested in setting up a 1,000 MT per day refinery in Hamriyah Free Zone and storage infrastructure of 27 tanks with 50,000 MT capacity. Capital work-in-progress recorded at AED 30.6M.

Company secured AED 63M in interest-bearing loans and borrowings from National Bank of Fujairah, comprising term loan (AED 16.7M), trust receipt facilities (AED 35.9M), and local bill discounting (AED 10.3M).
Company achieved revenue of AED 497M in 2024, up from AED 301M in 2023, representing 65% growth. Net profit increased 73% to AED 11.9M.
Company changed auditors from Behl, Lad & Al Sayegh to Baker Tilly MKM Chartered Accountants for 2024-2025 financial years.

market_size: Global petroleum trading market exceeds USD 2 trillion annually
sector_market_size: UAE is a major regional hub for petroleum trading with re-export value exceeding USD 50 billion annually
sector_market_size_comment: UAE benefits from strategic location, free zones, and advanced logistics infrastructure
sector_growth_comment: Growth driven by regional demand, expansion of refining capacity, and UAE's position as a trading hub between East and West
sector_local_share: UAE accounts for approximately 8-10% of Middle East petroleum trading volumes
sector_local_comment: Hamriyah Free Zone is a key strategic location for petroleum and chemicals trading and storage
sector_trade_flow: UAE imports crude and refined products from Asia, Middle East, and Americas; re-exports to Africa, South Asia, and Europe
sector_trade_comment: Strong import-export dynamics with favorable free zone regulations and tax incentives
sector_risks: Oil price volatility, geopolitical tensions, regulatory changes, competition from other regional hubs, environmental compliance requirements
sector_drivers: Strategic location, free zone benefits, growing regional demand, refining capacity expansion, logistics infrastructure
sector_major_players: Vitol, Trafigura, Gunvor, Glencore, plus numerous UAE-based trading companies
sector_summary_text: The UAE petroleum trading sector is characterized by high volumes, thin margins, and significant working capital requirements. Free zones like Hamriyah offer competitive advantages through tax benefits, 100% foreign ownership, and streamlined regulations. The sector is highly competitive with both multinational commodity traders and regional players. Success factors include access to financing, supplier relationships, logistics capabilities, and risk management expertise.
strengths: Strong revenue growth of 65% demonstrating market traction and operational scalability
Improving profitability metrics with gross margin expanding from 3.79% to 4.47% and net margin stable at 2.4%
Excellent ROE of 18.84% exceeding industry benchmarks, indicating efficient capital deployment
Strategic infrastructure investment in refining and storage facilities creating competitive moat and value-added capabilities
weaknesses: Rapid debt increase from near-zero to AED 63M creates financial risk and interest burden of AED 3.2M annually
Single shareholder concentration and minimal share capital (AED 11K) limit capital cushion in distress scenarios
Current ratio declined to 1.18 and quick ratio to 0.87, indicating tightening short-term liquidity
Thin net margins of 2.4% typical of commodity trading leave little room for adverse price movements or operational disruptions
opportunities: New refinery capacity (1,000 MT/day) and storage infrastructure (50,000 MT) enable higher-margin value-added services versus pure trading
Growing regional demand for refined products and petrochemicals supports volume expansion
Established relationships with major global suppliers (Gunvor, Trafigura) and diverse customer base provide business stability
Free zone location provides tax advantages, 100% foreign ownership, and streamlined logistics for international trade
threats: High leverage (debt-to-equity 0.99) increases vulnerability to adverse market conditions or commodity price volatility
Dependence on short-term credit facilities and working capital financing exposes company to refinancing risk
Commodity trading sector faces intense competition and margin pressure from larger well-capitalized players
Concentration of management in single individual creates key-person risk and succession planning concerns
recommended_payment_terms: 30-60 days with progressive limit increases based on payment performance
credit_opinion_text: APPROVE WITH CONDITIONS: Infinite Mining and Energy FZE demonstrates strong operational momentum with 65% revenue growth and improving profitability. The company has strategically invested in refining and storage infrastructure to move up the value chain beyond pure trading. However, the rapid debt accumulation (AED 63M), declining liquidity ratios, and single-shareholder structure warrant cautious approach. Recommended credit limit of USD 3.5M (approximately AED 13M, representing ~26% of annual revenue and ~35% of equity) with 30-60 day payment terms. Require: (1) Quarterly financial updates, (2) Notification of any debt covenant breaches, (3) Trade references from 2-3 existing suppliers, (4) Bank reference letter from National Bank of Fujairah. Monitor debt servicing capacity, working capital trends, and refinery utilization rates. Consider limit increases after 6 months of satisfactory payment performance. The company's established banking relationships, growing customer base, and strategic positioning in Hamriyah Free Zone support creditworthiness, but leverage levels require ongoing monitoring.

monitoring_triggers.trigger_event: Payment delay exceeding 15 days past due date
monitoring_triggers.trigger_action: Immediate account review, contact finance manager, hold further shipments until payment received
Request updated financial statements, reassess credit limit, consider requiring advance payment or bank guarantee
Schedule management meeting to discuss financial strategy, review debt covenants, potential limit reduction

monitoring_triggers.trigger_event: Late filing or qualified opinion on annual audited financials
monitoring_triggers.trigger_action: Investigate reasons, request management explanation, consider temporary credit hold pending resolution

monitoring_triggers.trigger_event: Material adverse change in ownership or key management
monitoring_triggers.trigger_action: Full credit review, meet new management, reassess risk profile and limits

monitoring_triggers.trigger_event: Negative news regarding legal disputes, regulatory violations, or banking relationship issues
monitoring_triggers.trigger_action: Immediate investigation, contact company for clarification, potential credit hold

data_quality_rating: Good - Based on audited financial statements (2022-2024), company questionnaire, and public registry information
data_limitations: Limited payment history with third parties. No independent trade references verified. Single-shareholder private company with limited public disclosures. Industry benchmarks are general estimates for petroleum trading sector. 2025 projections (AED 600M revenue) are management estimates and unverified.
data_source_analyst_comment: Financial data derived from audited statements by reputable firms (Baker Tilly MKM, Behl Lad & Al Sayegh). Company questionnaire completed by Finance Manager provides operational context. Recommend obtaining bank reference letter and 2-3 supplier trade references to validate payment behavior. Monitor actual 2025 performance against projections. Consider requiring personal guarantee from Mr. Bilal Iqbal Merchant given single-shareholder structure and recent leverage increase.
